findingDetails.title: Replay recovery remains manual despite documented rollback workflow
findingDetails.evidence: Recovery guidance tells operators to delete or ignore stale council-graph.sqlite rows and replay derived nodes/edges, while reviewed council graph DB and handlers expose only upsert/status/query/convergence paths and exact scoped searches found no delete/replay/reset implementation under lib/council-graph or handlers/council-graph.
findingDetails.recommendation: Add a bounded clear/rebuild helper or a documented replay command path that uses existing MCP surfaces, then cover stale-row recovery with a focused test.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Reviewed DB lifecycle, batchUpsert, handlers, tests, and graph_support.md recovery guidance; scoped exact searches for delete/replay/rollback/prune/clear/reset/remove under council graph lib and handlers returned no implementation hits.

noveltyJustification: One new P2 maintainability advisory was opened for rollback/replay ergonomics; no new P0/P1 was opened because source-of-truth artifacts remain authoritative and manual rebuild remains possible.
edgeCases: Findings registry remains stale relative to JSONL/prompt active findings; carry-forward uses state-log evidence until reducer refresh.
The first BINDING emission included a malformed target line before corrected canonical bindings; review scope stayed on the corrected resolved target.
Resource map is absent; resource-map coverage gate skipped.
ruledOut: No new P0/P1 maintainability issue was opened because the recovery gap is advisory while packet-local ai-council artifacts remain authoritative.
Did not retry exhausted SQL injection, cross-session read, or destructive source-of-truth concerns from prior iterations.
